title: Dynamic Glazing Tint Levels
summary: Dynamic glazing typically corresponds to four visible light transmittance levels.
evidence_quote: Typically, the tint corresponds to 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60% visible light transmittance Tvis depending on manufacturers.

title: Dynamic Glazing Application Feasibility
summary: Dynamic glazing is most feasible on large non-north-facing facades, especially east and west.
evidence_quote: Dynamic glazing is usually the most feasible on large glazing facades not do not face north. It works well on east and west orientations since these are difficult to horizontally shade.

title: Dynamic Glazing South Facade Use
summary: Dynamic glazing can be used efficiently on unshaded south facades.
evidence_quote: It could also be used efficiently on south facades that are not shaded.

title: Dynamic Glazing Control Method
summary: Dynamic glazing is controlled by low voltage current, either manually or via BMS.
evidence_quote: The dynamic glazing is usually controlled by a low voltage current and can be controlled either manually through a switch or linked into an automatic building management system (BMS).

title: Desiccant Dehumidification Dew Point Threshold
summary: Applications requiring a dew point at or below 40°F (4.3°C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.
evidence_quote: In general, applications that require a dew point at or below 40°F (4.3°C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.

title: Desiccant Process Air Temperature Rise
summary: Process air outlet temperature rises roughly in proportion to the amount of moisture removed.
evidence_quote: The outlet temperature rises roughly in proportion to the amount of moisture that is removed.

title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Operation
summary: Rotary dehumidifiers use a desiccant bed rotating between process and reactivation airstreams.
evidence_quote: Humid air, generally referred to as process air, is dehumidified in one part of the desiccant bed while a different part of the bed is dried for reuse by a second airstream known as reactivation air.

title: Solar Thermal Application Range
summary: Solar thermal applications range from low-temperature (pool heating, DHW) to medium/high-temperature (space heating, absorption cooling, steam production).
evidence_quote: Solar energy thermal applications range from low-temperature applications (swimming pool heating or domestic hot water) up to medium- or high-temperature applications (space heating, absorption cooling or steam production for electrical generation).

title: Solar Combi System Components
summary: A typical solar combi system for DHW and space heating includes collectors, storage tank, and auxiliary boiler.
evidence_quote: A typical installation for the combined production of DHW and space heating (i.e., solar combi systems) includes the solar collectors, the heat storage tank, and a boiler used as an auxiliary heater.
